skill.name: scientific-brainstorming
skill.description: Guides open-ended scientific brainstorming for early research planning, interdisciplinary exploration, assumption testing, and research gap discovery.
skill.summary: Structured ideation support for scientists exploring new research directions.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found prose-only brainstorming content at those locations. No executable code, command execution, filesystem access, network requests, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found. A low-risk marketplace concern remains because the skill suggests the author's external K-Dense Web platform for complex workflows.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are natural-language research brainstorming guidance and method descriptions. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashing algorithms, encryption settings, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are Markdown prose about ideation frameworks and research planning. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain ordinary English about doing less and adapting methods. They do not invoke host discovery, environment inspection, process listing, shell commands, or operating system APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is plain instructional text, not reconnaissance logic. No system information collection behavior appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: External Platform Promotion
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not malicious, but it may steer users toward an external hosted service and should be transparent to marketplace users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly names an external website and asks the assistant to suggest it in certain cases. There is no evidence of data exfiltration or automatic network access.

content.value_statement: Early research planning often stalls when ideas stay inside one discipline. This skill guides structured scientific brainstorming, assumption checks, and method exploration.
actual_capabilities: Guides open-ended research ideation through structured conversation.
Uses cross-domain analogies to connect scientific fields.
Applies methods such as SCAMPER, Six Thinking Hats, TRIZ, and biomimicry.
Challenges assumptions and reframes research problems.
Helps compare promising directions and identify practical next steps.
Suggests collaboration areas, pilot experiments, and literature search angles.
limitations: Does not validate scientific claims against current literature by itself.
Does not replace domain expert review or ethics approval.
Works best with user-provided context, constraints, and goals.
May produce speculative ideas that need feasibility screening.

use_cases.title: Generate Early Research Directions
use_cases.description: Explore broad research questions, possible mechanisms, and new study angles before formal hypothesis writing.

use_cases.title: Connect Methods Across Fields
use_cases.description: Find useful analogies, techniques, and collaborations from adjacent disciplines.

use_cases.title: Reframe Product Science Questions
use_cases.description: Turn vague technical challenges into testable research paths and pilot experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Brainstorm
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me brainstorm research directions for this topic: [topic]. Ask clarifying questions first, then suggest diverse angles.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge Assumptions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for reframing a known problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my current research framing: [framing]. Identify assumptions, reverse them, and suggest alternative study designs.

prompt_templates.title: Use Structured Methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply SCAMPER, Six Thinking Hats, and TRIZ to this research challenge: [challenge]. Compare the strongest ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for turning ideas into next steps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize these ideas into three research directions: [ideas]. For each, propose a pilot study, risks, and required expertise.

output_examples.input: Brainstorm new ways to study microbial community resilience after antibiotic exposure.
output: Cross-scale angle: compare single-species recovery with community-level rebound patterns.
Assumption reversal: ask whether transient instability predicts stronger long-term resilience.
Pilot study: time-series sequencing with controlled pulse exposures and recovery windows.

output_examples.input: Help reframe a materials science project on self-healing coatings.
output: Biomimicry path: compare wound closure, plant resin sealing, and shell repair strategies.
TRIZ contradiction: increase coating durability without reducing responsiveness.
Next step: map candidate mechanisms by trigger type, repair speed, and environmental tolerance.

best_practices: Provide the research field, current question, constraints, and available methods.
Separate divergent idea generation from feasibility review.
Ask for concrete next steps after the most promising ideas emerge.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a substitute for literature review.
Requesting final experimental protocols without expert validation.
Sharing sensitive unpublished data unless the environment is approved for it.

faq.question: What is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for early-stage research ideation, reframing problems, and exploring interdisciplinary connections.

faq.question: Can it generate testable hypotheses?
faq.answer: It can help explore hypothesis directions, but users should refine and validate hypotheses with domain evidence.

faq.question: Does it search current scientific literature?
faq.answer: No. It does not perform literature search unless the host tool provides separate browsing or retrieval features.

faq.question: Which brainstorming methods does it use?
faq.answer: It references SCAMPER, Six Thinking Hats, morphological analysis, TRIZ, biomimicry, provocation, and reverse assumptions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated research?
faq.answer: It can support ideation, but regulated work still needs expert review, compliance checks, and appropriate approvals.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the research topic, current model, constraints, available tools, and what kind of ideas you want.
